title: n8n Workflow Automation Platform Hit by Cascading Critical RCE Vulnerabilities: CISA Flags Active Exploitation, New Sandbox Escapes Enable Unauthenticated Credential Theft of AWS Keys and OAuth Tokens Across 24,700+ Exposed Instances (CVE-2025-68613, CVE-2026-27577, CVE-2026-27493)
affected_products: n8n workflow automation platform versions 1.65.0 to 1.120.3 (CVE-2025-68613 - actively exploited)
n8n workflow automation platform versions below 1.123.22 (CVE-2026-27577, CVE-2026-27493)
n8n workflow automation platform versions 2.0.0 to 2.9.2 (CVE-2026-27577, CVE-2026-27493)
n8n workflow automation platform version 2.10.0 (CVE-2026-27577, CVE-2026-27493)

timeline.event: Cyera Research Labs discovers and reports CVE-2026-21858 (Ni8mare) - unauthenticated RCE via Content-Type confusion in n8n webhooks with CVSS 10.0
n8n releases patch version 1.121.0 addressing Ni8mare; CVE-2025-68613 also patched in versions 1.120.4, 1.121.1, and 1.122.0
CVE-2026-21858 officially assigned; Cyera publishes Ni8mare disclosure revealing full CVSS 10.0 unauthenticated RCE affecting approximately 100,000 n8n servers globally
Supply chain attack discovered: 8 malicious npm packages mimicking legitimate n8n integrations deployed to steal OAuth tokens from developers
n8n releases patches for eight additional CVEs including CVE-2026-25049 (expression sandbox escape bypass that circumvents the CVE-2025-68613 fix)

CISA adds CVE-2025-68613 to Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog confirming active real-world exploitation; 24,700+ unpatched instances identified globally; federal compliance deadline set for March 25, 2026

Pillar Security discloses CVE-2026-27577 (expression compiler sandbox escape CVSS 9.4) and CVE-2026-27493 (form node double-evaluation unauthenticated RCE CVSS 9.5); n8n releases patches in versions 2.10.1, 2.9.3, and 1.123.22

simple_summary.what_happened: n8n, a popular open-source workflow automation platform used by tens of thousands of organizations to connect cloud services and automate business processes, has been hit by a cascade of critical security vulnerabilities over the past four months. Attackers have been exploiting these flaws in real-world attacks, confirmed by CISA adding CVE-2025-68613 to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog on March 12, 2026. The vulnerabilities allow attackers to steal stored credentials for AWS, Google, Salesforce, and hundreds of other connected services. Two new critical flaws disclosed today (CVE-2026-27577 and CVE-2026-27493) extend the attack surface to unauthenticated exploitation via public web forms.
simple_summary.who_is_affected: Any organization self-hosting n8n workflow automation platform on versions below 2.10.1. n8n is used by mid-market and enterprise organizations for workflow automation, connecting services including AWS, Azure, Salesforce, Google Workspace, Slack, payment processors, and internal databases. Organizations are especially at risk if their n8n instance is internet-accessible or stores integrations with sensitive cloud services.
simple_summary.what_attackers_do: Step 1: Attacker identifies internet-exposed n8n instances using Shodan or Censys - over 24,700 remain unpatched as of March 12, 2026. Step 2: For CVE-2026-27493, attacker submits a crafted payload to any public-facing n8n Form node endpoint (no authentication required), exploiting the double-evaluation bug where form fields starting with '=' are evaluated as expressions. Step 3: Attacker chains the form injection with CVE-2026-27577 (expression compiler sandbox escape) to break out of the sandbox and execute arbitrary OS commands. Step 4: Attacker reads the N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY environment variable from the server to decrypt all stored credentials in the n8n database. Step 5: All stored credentials are now compromised: AWS access keys, database passwords, Google OAuth tokens, Salesforce credentials, OpenAI API keys, and any other integration secrets stored in n8n.
what_you_should_do: Immediately upgrade n8n to version 2.10.1 - patches all known critical CVEs including the newly disclosed CVE-2026-27577 and CVE-2026-27493
Rotate all credentials stored in n8n integrations immediately - assume any AWS keys, OAuth tokens, database passwords, and API keys may have been exfiltrated
Restrict n8n network access to trusted internal networks and remove all internet-facing exposure
Set N8N_COMMUNITY_PACKAGES_ENABLED=false to prevent supply chain attacks via malicious npm packages
Disable public Form node endpoints or restrict them to authenticated users only
simple_summary.why_this_is_serious: n8n sits at the center of an organization's automation infrastructure, connected to virtually every sensitive service in the environment. A single compromised n8n instance can expose credentials for all connected cloud services simultaneously - not a single-service breach but a potential total credential compromise across an entire organization's cloud and SaaS footprint. The active exploitation confirmed by CISA, combined with 24,700+ exposed instances and four months of continuous new bypasses, indicates a determined attacker community actively targeting n8n deployments worldwide.

tactics.description: Attackers exploit CVE-2026-27493 form node double-evaluation via unauthenticated public Form endpoints, or CVE-2025-68613 expression injection, to gain initial code execution on internet-exposed n8n servers

CVE-2026-27577 expression compiler sandbox escape allows arbitrary OS command execution by exploiting a missing case in the AST rewriter that allows the process object to pass through the sandbox untransformed

Attackers access the N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY environment variable to decrypt all integration credentials stored in the n8n SQLite database, including AWS keys, OAuth tokens, database passwords, and API keys for connected services
Supply chain attack uses 8 malicious npm packages with names nearly identical to legitimate n8n community integrations to steal developer OAuth tokens without raising suspicion
Attackers read the n8n SQLite database (n8n.db) to extract all stored workflow credentials, API keys, and authentication tokens for connected integrations and cloud services
Stolen credentials (AWS keys, Salesforce tokens, database passwords, Google OAuth tokens) are used to authenticate into all cloud services and systems that n8n was previously authorized to access

detection_guidance.summary: Detection requires monitoring n8n application logs for suspicious expression patterns and Form node submissions with expression syntax, plus host-level monitoring for unexpected process execution and file access. Organizations should immediately audit n8n-connected cloud services for unauthorized API calls from unexpected source IPs as a sign of in-progress credential abuse.

log_sources.description: Monitor for expression evaluation errors, sandbox escape attempts, Form node submissions containing '=' prefixed values, and unexpected workflow triggers from external sources
Alert on unexpected child processes spawned by the n8n Node.js process, especially OS shell commands or file system access to sensitive locations outside the n8n data directory
Monitor access to the n8n SQLite database file (n8n.db) and N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY from any process other than n8n itself
Monitor n8n webhook and Form endpoints for HTTP request payloads starting with '=' character or containing JavaScript expression syntax in form field values

log_sources.source: Cloud Provider Access Logs (AWS CloudTrail, Google Cloud Audit, Azure Monitor)
log_sources.description: Monitor all cloud services connected to n8n for API calls from unexpected source IPs at unusual times - may indicate active use of stolen credentials

what_to_look_for.indicator: Form node submissions with '=' prefixed values in any input fields
what_to_look_for.description: CVE-2026-27493 double-evaluation bug: form submissions where any field begins with '=' character represent expression injection attempts via the unauthenticated form endpoint

what_to_look_for.indicator: References to process.env or process.exec in n8n expression evaluation context
what_to_look_for.description: CVE-2026-27577 sandbox escape: the process object should never appear in expression evaluation; any such reference in n8n logs indicates active exploitation

what_to_look_for.indicator: Unexpected file access to n8n.db SQLite database from non-n8n processes
what_to_look_for.description: Credential theft involves reading n8n.db; any non-n8n process accessing this file is a critical indicator of compromise requiring immediate investigation
what_to_look_for.log_field: file_path CONTAINS 'n8n.db' AND process_name NOT IN ['node', 'n8n']

what_to_look_for.indicator: New npm packages installed in n8n community nodes directory with unrecognized names
what_to_look_for.description: Supply chain attack installs malicious packages with names similar to legitimate n8n integrations; audit for packages not in the official n8n community nodes registry
what_to_look_for.log_field: npm_install_path CONTAINS 'n8n-nodes' AND package_name NOT IN approved_list

what_to_look_for.indicator: Cloud API calls from unexpected source IPs using n8n-associated service credentials
what_to_look_for.description: After credential theft, attackers use stolen keys from different infrastructure; check for n8n-managed credentials being used from IPs not matching the n8n server
what_to_look_for.log_field: cloud_api_source_ip NOT IN n8n_egress_ips AND credential_id IN n8n_stored_credentials

immediate_actions.description: Version 2.10.1 patches CVE-2026-27577 and CVE-2026-27493 (disclosed today) plus all prior critical CVEs. Legacy deployments should use 2.9.3 or 1.123.22 at minimum.

immediate_actions.action: Immediately rotate ALL credentials stored in n8n integrations
immediate_actions.description: Treat all AWS access keys, OAuth tokens, database passwords, and API keys stored in n8n as potentially compromised. Rotate all credentials for all connected services regardless of whether exploitation is confirmed.

immediate_actions.action: Remove internet-facing exposure of n8n and restrict to internal networks
immediate_actions.description: Block external access to n8n port 5678. Place behind VPN or firewall. For public Form nodes, implement strict server-side input validation to reject '=' prefixed field values.

immediate_actions.action: Disable community packages to block supply chain attack vector
immediate_actions.description: Set N8N_COMMUNITY_PACKAGES_ENABLED=false in n8n environment configuration to prevent installation of malicious npm packages.

immediate_actions.action: Audit all installed n8n community node packages against the official registry
immediate_actions.description: Review all installed n8n community packages. Remove any unrecognized packages or those using names similar to but not identical to known official integrations.
